column1: Which type of HTML element starts on a new line and extends the full width of the page?
Which type of HTML element only occupies the width of its content and does not start on a new line?
Name two examples of HTML block elements.
Name two examples of HTML inline elements.
In a CSS rule, what is the term for the part that selects the HTML element to be styled?
In a CSS rule, a combination of a property and a value is called a _____.
What is the term for multiple CSS declarations enclosed in curly brackets `{}`?
What are the three methods for including CSS in an HTML document?
column2: Inline CSS, Internal CSS, and External CSS.

column1: Which method of including CSS is considered best practice for managing styles across multiple pages?
column2: External CSS, using a `<link>` tag to a separate `.css` file.

column1: Why is inline CSS (using the `style` attribute) generally considered bad practice?
column2: Because you have to duplicate every style for every single tag, making it difficult to maintain.

column1: The CSS property `font-weight` sets the _____ of text characters.
What is the numeric value for a `normal` font-weight in CSS?
What is the numeric value for a `bold` font-weight in CSS, which is also the default for headings?
What CSS property is used to set decorative lines on text, such as an underline or line-through?
What is the primary use case for the `text-decoration` property in modern web design?
column2: To remove the default underline from anchor tags using the value `none`.

column1: The CSS property `font-style` is most commonly used to apply what style to text?
What is the purpose of the `list-style` property in CSS?
column2: It sets the style of the marker (e.g., bullet point) for a list item.

column1: What is the most common use case for the `list-style` property, especially for navigation bars?
column2: Setting it to `none` to remove default bullet points when lists are used for structural purposes.

column1: The CSS property `text-align` controls the horizontal alignment of text within which type of element?
Why does `text-align` have no effect on inline elements by default?
column2: Because an inline element's box is only as wide as its content, leaving no extra space for the text to move within.

column1: What does the `line-height` property control in CSS?
column2: The amount of space above and below inline elements, effectively setting the distance between lines of text.

column1: The CSS property _____ controls the space between individual characters in a block of text.
In CSS, what is the difference between absolute and relative units for sizing?
column2: Absolute units (like pixels) are fixed, while relative units are based on the size of a parent element and can adjust proportionally.

column1: What is a pixel (px) in the context of CSS?
column2: A single, fixed-size point of light on a digital display, representing an absolute unit of measurement.

column1: How do you import a font from Google Fonts into an HTML document?
column2: By copying the provided `<link>` tags from Google Fonts and pasting them into the `<head>` section of the HTML file.

column1: The CSS property for setting the color of text is simply `_____`.
What color system, commonly used in electronic displays and CSS, starts with black and combines red, green, and blue light?
What does a hexadecimal (hex) color code in CSS represent?
column2: It represents a color by specifying the intensity of Red, Green, and Blue (RGB) components with a `#` followed by six characters.

column1: In the context of CSS, what is the 'Cascade'?
column2: The principle that styles declared later in the stylesheet will take priority over earlier styles if the selectors are the same.

column1: How can you apply the same CSS styles to multiple selectors (e.g., `h1`, `h2`, `h3`) without repeating the code?
column2: By grouping the selectors with a comma-separated list.

column1: What is the CSS syntax for selecting an element with a specific ID, for example, an ID of `main-title`?
How many times can a specific ID be used on a single HTML page?
What is the CSS syntax for selecting elements with a specific class, for example, a class of `highlight`?
Why are class selectors generally preferred over ID selectors for styling?
column2: Because classes can be reused on multiple elements, offering greater flexibility and scalability.

column1: What is the purpose of a pseudo-class like `:hover` in CSS?
column2: It applies styles to an element only when it is in a specific state, such as when the user's mouse pointer is over it.

column1: Which pseudo-class is used to style a link that the user has already visited?
What determines which CSS rule is applied when multiple, conflicting rules target the same element using different selectors?
Which type of selector has the highest specificity: ID, class, or type (element)?
What is CSS inheritance?
column2: The process where properties set on a parent element are passed down to its child elements by default.

column1: Which CSS properties are commonly inherited by child elements?
What is the best practice for setting global font styles that should be inherited by most text elements on a page?
column2: Set the styles on the `body` element.

column1: The _____ selector, denoted by an asterisk (`*`), applies styles to every single element on the page.
What are the four main components of the CSS Box Model, from the inside out?
What is 'padding' in the CSS Box Model?
column2: The space between the element's content and its border.

column1: What is 'margin' in the CSS Box Model?
column2: The space outside an element's border, separating it from other elements.

column1: The CSS property `background-color` applies color to which parts of the box model?
By default, do standard CSS `width` and `height` properties apply to inline elements like `<span>` and `<a>`?
column2: No, `width` and `height` cannot be set on most inline elements.

column1: What is the shorthand property in CSS to set the width, style, and color of a border all at once?
The CSS property `border-radius` is used to create what visual effect on an element's corners?
What is the default behavior of `box-sizing: content-box`?
column2: The `width` and `height` properties apply only to the content area; padding and border are added on top, increasing the element's total size.

column1: What is the effect of setting `box-sizing: border-box`?
column2: The `width` and `height` properties define the total size of the element, including content, padding, and border.

column1: What is a common 'CSS reset' rule applied with the universal selector (`*`)?
How can you horizontally center a block-level element that has a set width within its parent container?
column2: By setting its left and right margins to `auto` (e.g., `margin: 0 auto;`).

column1: What is the key difference between the `width` and `max-width` properties?
column2: `width` sets a fixed width, while `max-width` sets a maximum width that the element will not exceed but allows it to shrink on smaller screens.

column1: Why is it best practice to use `max-width` instead of `width` for main page containers?
column2: To ensure the container is responsive and its content does not overflow on screens narrower than the specified width.

column1: What does the `display: inline-block;` value do?
column2: It allows an element to flow inline with other content like an inline element, but also allows `width`, `height`, padding, and margin to be applied like a block element.

column1: A percentage (%) unit in CSS is always relative to what?
column2: A property of its parent element.

column1: What is the `rem` unit in CSS relative to?
column2: The font size of the root element (`<html>`).

column1: What is the common practice of setting `font-size: 62.5%` on the `html` element intended to achieve?
column2: It makes 1rem equal to 10 pixels (since the default is 16px), simplifying calculations.

column1: Where are CSS variables (custom properties) typically defined to make them globally available?
What is the syntax for defining a CSS variable named `primary-color`?
How do you use a CSS variable named `--primary-color` to set the background of an element?
To initialize a flexbox layout, what CSS property and value must be applied to the parent container?
In a flexbox layout, the direct children of the flex container are known as _____.
What does the `flex-direction` property control in a flexbox layout?
column2: It establishes the main axis, determining if flex items are arranged in a row or a column.

column1: The `justify-content` property aligns flex items along which axis?
Which `justify-content` value places flex items at the end of the main axis?
Which `justify-content` value distributes flex items evenly, with the first and last items flush against the container edges?
The `align-items` property aligns flex items along which axis?
Which `align-items` value will vertically center items in a default row-based flex container?
By default, what is the value of `align-items`, which causes flex items to stretch and fill the height of the container?
What is the purpose of the `gap` property in a flex container?
column2: It sets the space between flex items, both horizontally and vertically.
